title: New flight ticket refund & cancellation rules effective today: How will passengers benefit?
content: The change in refund rules underlines the ‘minimum requirements for refund of ticket’. This includes tickets for both domestic and international travel, booked with domestic and international airlines operating to and from India.

title: Middle East crisis: PM Modi to hold talks with CMs tomorrow, review preparedness
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will convene a virtual meeting with chief ministers on Friday to assess India's preparedness for the ongoing Middle East conflict. The discussion will focus on coordinating national and state-level responses, particularly concerning supply chains, energy, and emergency systems. States under the model code of conduct will have a separate meeting.

title: BCCI unveils India’s 2026-27 home fixtures: 22 matches across 17 cities
content: Team India gears up for a massive 2026-27 home cricket season, hosting West Indies, Sri Lanka, Zimbabwe, and Australia in 22 matches across 17 cities. The packed schedule features ODIs, T20Is, and culminates with a five-Test Border-Gavaskar Trophy against Australia, promising thrilling contests nationwide.

title: 'Excessive noise' forces London-bound AI flight to divert after 7 hours in air
content: Air India's new Airbus A350, VT-JRF, faced a recurring issue with turbulence and noise in its belly hold, forcing a return to Delhi from a London-bound flight. This marks the second such incident in ten days, prompting a DGCA investigation. The aircraft had previously diverted to Shannon for checks after a similar problem on a New York to Delhi route.

title: Watch: Strangers, a dog, and a moment the world chose to remember
content: A heartwarming act of kindness in Almaty, Kazakhstan, where strangers formed a human chain to rescue a dog from a reservoir in 2016, has been immortalized in a bronze sculpture. Unveiled recently, the artwork celebrates solidarity and collective action, reigniting online discussions about humanity's capacity for compassion and reminding us of the value in simple, selfless gestures.

title: Indian-origin man charged in Singapore for driving car with children on rear boot
content: A man of Indian origin faces charges in Singapore. He allegedly drove a luxury car with his two young children sitting on the rear boot. This dangerous act put the children's lives at risk. Authorities have stated that such behavior is unacceptable and violates traffic rules. Strict action will be taken against those who endanger lives.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 8: Ranveer Singh film eyes 'Dhurandhar 1' record of Rs 840
content: As Aditya Dhar’s espionage masterpiece Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge enters its eighth day on Thursday, March 26, 2026, the Ranveer Singh starrer is rewriting the history of Indian cinema. Having already shattered the Rs. 1,000 crore worldwide milestone in record time, the film is now tracking at nearly double the efficiency of previous blockbusters like 'Jawan', 'Pathaan', and 'Animal.' Despite its sprawling 3-hour and 49-minute runtime, the sequel's domestic net collection has surged to more than Rs. 630 crore, fueled by unprecedented demand for midnight screenings and a gripping narrative that sees Jaskirat Singh Rangi, played by Ranveer Singh deeper than ever into the Karachi underworld as Hamza Ali Mazari.

title: OnePlus shutdown fears grow as India CEO exits: Here is what the company is saying
content: OnePlus India operations are under scrutiny following CEO Robin Liu's resignation. While the company assures business continuity, reports suggest a restructuring where Liu now reports to Realme's CEO. This comes after Liu previously denied shutdown rumors, and as OnePlus faces significant shipment declines in the Indian market, raising questions about its future.

title: Bab al-Mandeb: How the 'Gate of Tears' may emerge as Iran’s second choke point after Hormuz
content: Iran has issued a stern warning, threatening to disrupt the vital Bab al-Mandeb Strait if the US and Israel attempt a ground invasion of Iranian territory, including Kharg Island. This move, coupled with existing control over the Strait of Hormuz, could significantly escalate economic disruption. Iran-aligned Houthis in Yemen are reportedly poised to join this effort, expanding the conflict's reach.

title: Did Israel drop Iran's Araghchi, Qalibaf from its hit list on Pak's request?
content: Pakistan claims it successfully intervened with Washington to prevent Israel from targeting senior Iranian officials, Foreign Minister Abbas Aragchi and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf. Islamabad argued their elimination would leave no one for de-escalation talks. This follows reports of Israel granting temporary immunity to the Iranian figures during ongoing negotiations.

title: America's largest bank CEO reveals work from home trend he didn't notice earlier
content: JP Morgan CEO Jamie Dimon criticizes remote work, citing a lack of attentiveness in virtual meetings and its detrimental impact on young employees' learning and emotional intelligence. He argues that in-office presence is crucial for skill development and project ownership, contrasting it with the 'game playing' and reduced accountability he observes in remote setups.

title: US weighs redirecting Ukraine-bound weapons to Middle East: Report
content: The Pentagon is reportedly considering rerouting some weapons, including air defence interceptors, originally destined for Ukraine to the Middle East due to strains on US stockpiles from the ongoing Iran war. This move highlights the difficult choices Washington faces in balancing support for Kyiv with its operational needs in the region.

title: Govt assures strong fuel security: No LPG shortage, crude secured, PNG transition
content: India's fuel supply remains stable and secure. The government assures citizens that petrol, diesel, and LPG are readily available nationwide. Officials are combating a misinformation campaign designed to create panic. Strategic reserves are robust, covering approximately 60 days of supply. Domestic production and imports ensure continuous availability of essential fuels.

title: ‘Not as spicy anymore’: AB de Villiers flags concern over MI star ahead of IPL
content: Former South Africa captain AB de Villiers has expressed concern for Mumbai Indians ahead of IPL 2026, suggesting Trent Boult may have lost pace and effectiveness. De Villiers warned that this could be an area opposition batters might target, despite the team's otherwise strong squad.  However, Abhinav Mukund remains optimistic about Mumbai's depth and balance.

title: LPG vs PNG explained: What's the difference between these fuels?
content: Global energy market tensions, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz, have highlighted the vulnerabilities of LPG supply to India. In contrast, piped natural gas (PNG) remains largely unaffected due to its integrated pipeline network, offering greater supply security for households and businesses.

title: 'Always the villain in the story': Faf du Plessis' unique take on Gautam Gambhir
content: Former South African captain Faf du Plessis lauded Gautam Gambhir's leadership, calling him a 'villain' for rivals but a respected figure for his team.  Du Plessis highlighted Gambhir's high standards and accountability, evident in his successful stints with KKR and India's recent limited-overs triumphs, despite Test match struggles.

title: Toll system in Hormuz? Iran drafts bill to charge ships for safe passage amid war
content: The Middle East war has intensified an oil crisis as Iran's actions in the Strait of Hormuz disrupt global energy supplies.  While the Red Sea's Bab el-Mandeb Strait is considered an alternative, it also faces threats. Reopening Hormuz is complicated by its geography and requires significant security measures, including a multinational coalition, to overcome the risks of attacks and minefields.

title: Bengaluru professor proposes to girl in classroom; student hits him with slipper
content: Chaos erupted at Sri Siddhartha Institute of Medical Sciences after Professor Mohammed allegedly proposed to a student in class, claiming she reciprocated his feelings. The student vehemently denied this, leading to a heated exchange and a physical altercation where she reportedly assaulted him. The incident, captured on video, has sparked widespread outrage.

title: Why your IGNOU degree may be rendered invalid
content: IGNOU continues to admit students to its MA Psychology program despite a UGC ban on distance learning for courses requiring clinical training. This move potentially invalidates degrees for thousands of applicants, including a teacher who paid fees and attended classes, raising concerns about regulatory compliance and student futures.

title: 'They’ll get mowed down': Did Trump reject Netanyahu’s plan to urge Iranians to protest?
content: US president Trump reportedly rejected Israeli prime minister Netanyahu's proposal for a joint public call for Iranians to protest, fearing high civilian casualties. While Israel prioritizes fostering an uprising, the US views regime change as a secondary outcome. This divergence emerged after Israeli strikes targeting key Iranian security figures, aimed at creating conditions for popular dissent.

title: 'Delhi's Rehman Dakait': BJP's 'Dhurandhar' dig at Kejriwal over 'sheesh mahal'
content: Delhi PWD minister Parvesh Sahib Singh Verma sparked a political storm by comparing former CM Arvind Kejriwal to a robber over the renovation of his official residence. A CAG report highlighted a significant cost escalation for the bungalow, dubbed a 'sheesh mahal' by the BJP.

title: Yograj slams Abhishek Sharma: ‘You fail because your mind is somewhere else’
content: Former India cricketer Yograj Singh has sharply criticised young opener Abhishek Sharma following India's T20 World Cup 2026 victory. Singh questioned Abhishek's focus and discipline, citing off-field distractions and an inconsistent tournament performance. He emphasised the need for rigorous training and a disciplined approach, drawing parallels with his son Yuvraj Singh's work ethic.

title: Indore horror: Techie killed as car rams residents in penthouse row
content: A dispute over commercial penthouse rentals in Indore's Sagar Township tragically turned deadly. A man allegedly drove his car into a group of residents during an argument, killing software engineer Shampa Pathak and injuring others. The conflict arose from residents' objections to short-term rentals causing security concerns. Both the driver and his father are in custody.

title: Why IPL 2026 could unleash Virat Kohli’s most dangerous version yet
content: Virat Kohli, IPL's leading run-scorer, finally achieved his dream of winning the IPL trophy with Royal Challengers Bengaluru in 2025. Now, unburdened by expectations after relinquishing captaincy, he enters IPL 2026 with a rare sense of freedom, potentially unleashing his most dangerous form yet.

title: I eat non-veg even on Ugadi and Shivaratri: CM Siddaramaiah
content: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah sparked debate by stating he eats non-vegetarian food on festival days like Ugadi and Maha Shivaratri, asserting personal belief over ritual. He also explained presenting the budget before an inauspicious time was a family and official suggestion, a rare deviation from his usual disregard for such beliefs.

title: How will UDAN 2.0 scheme benefit consumers? Check key features of the aviation initiative
content: India's UDAN 2.0 scheme, with a Rs 28,000 crore outlay, aims to significantly boost regional air travel over 10 years. The revamped initiative will develop 100 airports, support aerodrome operations, build helipads, and provide financial aid to airlines, enhancing connectivity to Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.

title: Trump says he wants Iran war to end within weeks: Report
content: President Trump aims to conclude the Iran conflict within weeks, believing it's in its final stages. He has urged officials to adhere to a four-to-six-week timeline, potentially before a summit with China's Xi Jinping. White House states Iran expressed willingness to talk, leading to postponed strikes. However, Tehran insists hostilities will cease only on its terms, vowing continued defence

title: Vijay Mallya on RCB sale: ‘Will remain part of my DNA’, recalls Virat Kohli pick
content: Former RCB owner Vijay Mallya expressed his emotions following the franchise's record sale, stating it remains in his DNA. He recalled his initial INR 450 crore investment in 2008, which has now grown to an astounding INR 16,600 crore. Mallya highlighted his decision to pick a young Virat Kohli as a significant memory, thanking fans for their unwavering support.

title: Royal Enfield Guerrilla 450 update teased: Launch tomorrow, what to expect
content: Royal Enfield is set to unveil an updated Guerrilla 450 on March 27, 2026. While the core design and 452cc engine remain, expect enhanced riding dynamics with grippier tires and revised suspension. New color options are likely, and features like traction and cruise control could be added. This refresh aims to boost its appeal.

title: ‘I’m back bowling’: Cummins eyes IPL return, backs Ishan Kishan as SRH captain
content: Australia captain Pat Cummins is targeting a mid-IPL 2026 return after resuming bowling following a back injury. He confirmed a structured rehabilitation plan aimed for his availability for the latter half of the tournament and finals. In his absence, Ishan Kishan will lead Sunrisers Hyderabad, a decision Cummins fully supports.

title: 7th case against astrologer: Woman accuses Ashok Kharat of rape on promise of bright career
content: Astrologer Ashok Kharat faces a seventh rape case in Nashik after a 24-year-old woman accused him of drugging and sexually assaulting her multiple times. Kharat allegedly threatened the victim with his 'divine powers' to ensure her silence. This latest complaint follows his arrest on March 17, with a special investigation team now probing further allegations.

title: 'Friendly nations' only: Iran allows India, Pakistan, 3 other countries to use Strait of Hormuz
content: Iran has opened the Strait of Hormuz for friendly nations like India, China, Russia, Iraq, and Pakistan. This comes as UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres urged an end to the conflict and the reopening of the vital waterway. The Strait of Hormuz is a crucial oil route, and its volatility impacts global energy flows amid regional tensions.

title: Murray drops 53, Jokic nearly has 20-20-20 game
content: Jamal Murray scored 53 points and Nikola Jokic had 23 points, 21 rebounds and 19 assists as the Nuggets held off the Mavericks on Wednesday night.

title: Draymond: Hated and loved Wemby's MVP case
content: Warriors forward Draymond Green said he both "hated" and "absolutely loved" the MVP self-promotion from Spurs center Victor Wembanyama.

title: Brown-led Celts hand Thunder first loss in month
content: Jaylen Brown scored 14 of his 31 points in a pivotal third quarter, and the Boston Celtics rallied to beat the Oklahoma City Thunder 119-109 on Wednesday night, snapping the defending champions' 12-game win streak.

title: Nacua sued over alleged antisemitic remark, bite
content: A woman in Los Angeles has filed a civil lawsuit against Puka Nacua alleging the Rams wide receiver made an "unprovoked antisemitic statement" and later bit her on the shoulder.

title: Fried's gem lifts Yanks despite Judge's 4-K opener
content: Max Fried allowed just two hits in 6⅓ scoreless innings, and the Yankees cruised past the Giants 7-0 on Wednesday night despite Aaron Judge's first career hitless Opening Day.

title: Arizona's Lloyd brushes off UNC job speculation
content: Amid speculation he could be a candidate for the vacancy at North Carolina, Arizona coach Tommy Lloyd insisted all his attention is with his program ahead of Thursday's Sweet 16 game against Arkansas.

title: Macdonald: JSN deal rewards 'foundational' player
content: Seahawks GM John Schneider and coach Mike Macdonald said they felt comfortable giving Jaxon Smith-Njigba his record-setting extension because of his "unique combination" of excellence on and off the field.

title: Flacco: Teams 'dumb' for not signing me to start
content: Joe Flacco is back with the Bengals as Joe Burrow's backup, but he isn't shy about voicing his belief that teams made a mistake by passing on him to be their starting quarterback.

content: A'ja Wilson will likely get the $1.4 million supermax, while Caitlin Clark's rookie contract will pay over $500,000.

content: From freshman phenoms to seasoned veterans, here are the stars who delivered head-turning performances.

title: 'The epitome of Texas basketball': These are Rori Harmon's Longhorns
content: The Texas point guard has played five seasons while under constant pressure from her coach, Vic Schaefer. It has made both of them better.

title: Ranking the remaining 16 teams in the women's NCAA tournament
content: Six SEC teams and five from the ACC (including a 10-seed!) make up the Sweet 16. Here's how they stack up.

title: Man United's big night was disappointing, but not their spirit
content: It was a huge night for Manchester United in the UEFA Women's Champions League, but they will need to bounce back next week to make it even more historic.

title: Forget Ronaldo, Rooney or Henry: Salah is the Premier League's greatest-ever player
content: Cristiano Ronaldo has five Ballon d'Ors and Thierry Henry will forever be an "Invincible," but neither had a Premier League career to match Mohamed Salah's.

title: Cost of the World Cup: How much might a USMNT fan spend to follow their team?
content: From tickets to hotels, how expensive is this summer's big event? Let's find out -- through the eyes of a USMNT fan on the trip of a lifetime.

title: World Cup playoff preview, predictions: Who will book the final six spots?
content: The final six spots -- four from Europe and two from the intercontinental playoff -- will be clinched this week.

title: What's next for Florida, Kansas, Kentucky, UNC: Recruits, transfer portal, more
content: Their NCAA tournament runs ended early and now it's all about next season. Here's how it's taking shape.

title: UConn star Azzi Fudd and the family behind her final March
content: Azzi Fudd's "imperfectly perfect" family has been her rock through ACL tears, dog food pranks and Beyoncé on repeat. Now they're hoping for one more NCAA tournament title for UConn.

title: J. Cole breaks down why LeBron James was carrying his latest 'The Fall-Off' vinyl
content: The Los Angeles Lakers star brought the vinyl for the rapper to sign while James met Cole's children.

title: Men's tournament: Three bets and two Eliminator picks for the round of 16
content: How to bet on top-2 seeds Purdue, Houston and Duke in Thursday's and Friday's regional semifinal games.

title: Women's tournament: UConn still title favorite as Virginia upset shakes up the odds
content: Odds for each team to advance through the bracket and to win the 2026 women's college basketball championship.

title: Max Verstappen vs. F1's new rules: Every complaint this season -- and our verdict
content: Amid a wave of complaints from F1's four-time world champion Max Verstappen, ESPN's Nate Saunders examines every one and gives his verdict on if they are warranted.

title: DGCA asks Air India to take corrective actions after wrong plane deployment to Vancouver
content: A Vancouver-bound Air India Boeing 777-200 LR aircraft, after being airborne for over seven hours, returned to Delhi on March 19 after it was found that the plane was not approved for operating the flight

title: India has 60 days of fuel stocks, government says dismissing shortage reports as misinformation
content: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas said all petrol pumps across the country are adequately stocked and operating normally, with no rationing of petrol or diesel

title: Iran War tests European unity amid German economic slowdown and fears over Ukraine support
content: The stand of EU member states in the conflict is divergent as Europe faces economic slowdown and inflation amid rising oil and gas prices; Germany fears opposing U.S.-Israel actions could prompt U.S. to withdraw support from Ukraine, while the war could hurt its already faltering economy more

title: Bus-truck collision in Markapuram LIVE: Recurring bus infernos  highlight regulatory gaps, trigger safety review
content: Expressing his heartfelt sympathies, Prime Minister Modi stated that an ex-gratia of ₹2 lakh will be provided to the next of kin of each deceased

title: Fuel crisis LIVE: India has 60 days of fuel stocks, no supply shortage, says government
content: Nayara Energy raised petrol prices by ₹5 per litre and diesel by ₹3 a litre, passing on part of the recent surge in global oil prices following the war in West Asia

title: Markapuram bus-truck collision: Andhra Pradesh Ministers express grief
content: A major road accident took place near Rayavaram in Markapuram district of Andhra Pradesh’s Prakasam district in the wee hours on March 26, 2026

title: Assembly Elections LIVE updates: Illicit inducements worth over ₹400 crore seized in 4 States and UT in the last month, ECI says
content: Electronic Seizure Management System (ESMS) was activated on February 26; SDPI pulls out of Manjeswaram contest, easing vote-split concerns for UDF

title: Hotel cashier allegedly refused to serve food to couple in Hassan, made derogatory remarks about religion
content: Police registered a case against the cashier for deliberate and malicious acts intended to outrage religious feelings and wounding the religious feelings of any class of citizens

title: PMK founder Ramadoss releases election manifesto, promises caste-based census
content: The 101-page manifesto, released by Mr. Ramadoss, outlined several promises, including quality, compulsory, and free education

title: Seven Indian fishermen from Rameswaram arrested by Sri Lankan Navy
content: The Sri Lankan Navy personnel detained two boats, in which, seven fishermen were engaged in fishing near Neduntheevu islet

title: Rotten eggs used to make cakes and donuts, low-quality coconut powder repackaged; illegal food units busted in Hyderabad
content: Three brothers were arrested for manufacturing and selling adulterated ginger and garlic paste. Total seven arrested in series of crackdowns.

title: Recurring bus infernos in Andhra Pradesh highlight regulatory gaps, trigger safety review
content: Accidents of this intensity involving human lives have been occurring in quick succession calling for swift action to prevent such tragedies

title: Kerala polls: Expelled IUML leader Irshad booked over ‘misogynistic’ remarks against CPI(M) legislator U. Prathibha
content: Alex Varghese, District Collector and Election Officer of Alappuzha, conducted a preliminary inquiry into a complaint from the CPI(M) mandalam committee and found a prima facie violation of Model Code of Conduct regarding the remarks

title: Ban use of toxic chemicals in sanitary pads, baby diapers: PIL plea in Madras High Court
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan call for the response of the Ministries of Health and Family Welfare and Textiles

title: Only for IPL fans: Check entry and exit plan for matches at Chinnaswamy stadium in Bengaluru; police issue traffic advisory, urge maximum use of BMTC buses and Namma Metro
content: Dedicated pick-up and drop-off points for app-based cabs and autos have been arranged at B.R.V. Ground (near Gate No. 06), and Manekshaw Parade Ground (near Gate No. 03)

title: How an ex-IPS officer, transferred by Election Commission for praising Jayalalithaa, later became AIADMK MLA
content: Nearly 20 months after R. Natraj joined the AIADMK, he was taken aback when Jayalalithaa expelled him from the party and asked the cadre not to have any relations with him.

title: PM Modi expresses condolences over Markapur road accident, announces ex-gratia
content: Authorities are continuing rescue and relief operations, and the injured have been shifted to nearby hospitals for treatment

title: Police Complaints Authority seeks crackdown on vehicles causing noise pollution in Bengaluru
content: KSPCA member Mohan Kumar Danappa highlighted the increasing nuisance caused by two-wheelers and other vehicles using prohibited or defective silencers, particularly in Bengaluru and other urban areas

title: Palaniswami has no right to comment on DMK front’s seat-sharing: Minister Sivasankar
content: In a video message posted on social media, another DMK leader and School Education Minister Anbil Mahesh Poyyamozhi said the AIADMK had “mortgaged” itself to the BJP and forgotten the welfare of the people.

title: Air India’s A350 aircraft enroute to London faces technical issue; returns to Delhi
content: The same A350-900 aircraft VT-JRF had faced a technical issue on March 15, following which the plane operating the flight from New York to Delhi was diverted to the Irish town of Shannon

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Touted as cement hub, but for Ariyalur constituency voters the future appears grey
content: Residents, who blame successive governments for failing to invest adequately and prioritise the district, feel that the constituency would have changed to a great extent if it had high-profile leaders

title: Transgender persons Amendment Bill opposed by community collectives
content: The Bill, passed by the Lok Sabha through a voice vote and now pending consideration in the Rajya Sabha, removes the provision for self-identification of gender, a principle recognised by the Supreme Court in the 2014 NALSA judgment and the 2014 Puttaswamy privacy ruling

title: Amidst waving of party flags, an offbeat representation of the Flag above all flags
content: Against the backdrop of intense poll activity, a 400-metre stretch of an arterial road in the outskirts of Chennai (technically in Madurapakkam panchayat under Tambaram assembly constituency) presents a salute to the idea of res publica, which underpins India’s system of governance, where citizens have a say, at least before a government is securely ensconced in the seat of power.  A Republic Day initiative shines bright every night on a 400-metre stretch of a road in Kovilancherry

title: Kerala Assembly polls: SDPI pulls out of Manjeswaram contest, easing vote-split concerns for UDF
content: SDPI’s withdrawal of its candidate comes days after criticism that the party’s presence in the fray could divide votes and indirectly aid BJP

title: 12,702 verdicts: What Telangana’s villages said about Congress, BRS and BJP
content: The BRS defection factor, pro-incumbency, better performance among tribal people -- these factors aided in the Congress’ facile victory in the recently held rural body polls in Telangana

title: Woman and infant feared dead in Markapuram bus accident
content: The construction workers from Andhra Pradesh mainly depend on the private bus services to commute to their native places from various towns in north Telangana

title: A Kodandarama temple that may have inspired Muthuswami Dikshitar’s raganga composition
content: Located in Vaduvur on the banks of river Vennar, the temple with its tranquil setting and layered history is said to have a link to the composer’s Kokilaravam kriti.

title: All private buses should adhere to safety norms: Deputy Transport Commissioner
content: The Vizianagaram Deputy Transport Commissioner said that the department had been conducting inspections in nearly 50 private buses plying to various destinations

title: Kerala polls: Pinarayi Vijayan slams Rahul Gandhi, Congress for ‘aiding BJP to come to power in various States’
content: Pinarayi Vijayan alleges that Rahul Gandhi, ‘though a national leader of Congress, does not even match up to local leaders of his party in terms of his competence to comprehend various issues’

title: LPG crisis: Cooking gas crunch revives firewood economy in rural and semi-urban Karnataka
content: The surge in demand, triggered by supply constraints linked to the ongoing West Asia crisis, has given rise to a parallel, largely informal firewood market in many districts

title: Queues thin on the third day at Hyderabad’s fuel outlets
content: Traffic Police reported a significant reduction in congestion near fuel stations compared to the previous two days

title: Major fire breaks out at oil recycling firm in Kerala’s Edayar; 1 killed
content: Victim identified as a Bihar native. The blaze is believed to have originated from a thermic fluid heater

content: The gun battle, which lasted for around 30 minutes, started after suspected Kuki militants opened fire at an Army post on Wednesday night (March 25, 2026)

content: Congress said Pakistan can even be so considered for a mediating role, is the most damning indictment of both the substance and style of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's diplomacy, which has been full of bombast and marked by cowardice

content: Raids by Counter Intelligence Wing of Jammu and Kashmir Police target key locations linked to a terror network orchestrated by Lashkar-e-Taiba operatives

content: Cauvery faces potential “near-term decline” of about 3.5% of its waters between 2026 and 2050, even as its northern counterparts brace for floods; Godaveri-Cauvery link project may be a critical solution

content: Telangana Civil Supplies Department on Tuesday (March 24, 2026) attributed the ‘no stock’ boards at some of the outlets to sudden panic driven buying

content: Global oil and gas prices have surged after Iran virtually blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow shipping lane between the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman, that handles roughly 20% of global oil and LNG

content: It's never too early to start thinking about fantasy football -- especially during a tumultuous offseason.

title: When drinking water became a revolution | Mahad Satyagraha
content:
A century ago, Ambedkar led a protest in Mahad that turned the right to water into a fight for dignity and equality.
When drinking water became an act of resistance, this is the story of the Mahad Satyagraha. 100 years after Mahad, Ambedkar’s fight for equality still resonates across India.

title: Misinformation wave deepens anxiety in Hyderabad, triggers panic buying and citywide disruption

title: Only a structured party combined with popularity can reach success: Thousand Lights MLA N. Ezhilan
content: Thousand Lights MLA from the DMK N. Ezhilan, in an interview to The Hindu, observes that any popular personality entering politics will attract the limelight. But only a structured party machinery combined with popularity can ensure success. In this interview, he speaks about his tenure as first-time MLA from Thousand Lights Assembly constituency in Chennai, Vijay’s political entry shaping the 2026 elections, redressal of key grievances and more.

title: Four queer voices explain the impact of the Trans Amendment Bill 2026
content: The Transgender Amendment Bill which was passed in Lok Sabha on March 24 impacts a trans person’s ability to access better education, health care, and the right to self-determination, say gender-diverse members who took part in a nation-wide protest in Chennai on March 23. They strongly call for repealing the amendment
